purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines the business logic and semantic model for order items, including revenue calculations, food/drink item classification, and related metrics. It serves as a mart layer component that aggregates and structures data about individual items within customer orders for analytical reporting.

evidence.description: Semantic model defining order item entities, dimensions, and measures including revenue calculations
Metrics definitions including simple, ratio, derived, and cumulative revenue metrics
YAML structure defining mart-level business logic for order item analytics
YAML key models
YAML key unit_tests
YAML key semantic_models

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across the organization.

evidence.description: Macro definition shows business logic for converting cents to dollars with database-specific implementations
Transformation dbt_model reads none and writes model.cents_to_dollars

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It determines order characteristics like whether an order contains food or drink items and calculates totals for cost, price, and item counts.

evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE aggregates supply costs, product prices, and counts food/drink items per order
compute_booleans CTE joins orders with item summaries and derives is_food_order/is_drink_order flags
customer_order_count CTE adds customer_order_number using window function ordered by timestamp
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_orders, model.order_items and writes model.orders
Import reference to order_items
Import reference to orders
Import reference to order_items_summary
dbt reference stg_orders

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing key order details like totals, timestamps, and item breakdowns (food/drink). It supports downstream reporting through semantic models, metrics, and saved queries for business insights.

evidence.description: Model definition with columns describing order attributes such as order_total, ordered_at, is_food_order, is_drink_order
Unit tests validating transformation logic from order items to boolean flags for food/drink orders
Semantic model defining entities and measures for order-based analytics
Metrics definitions including order_total, new_customer_orders, food_orders, drink_orders
Saved query aggregating multiple metrics by time dimension for reporting purposes

evidence.description: Macro implements business rules for schema naming with conditional logic for seeds, custom schemas, and production vs non-production environments

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product. It provides enriched order item data including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply chain costs for business analysis.

evidence.description: Aggregation of supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Join logic combining order items with orders, products, and supply cost data
Selection of product attributes (product_name, product_price, is_food_item, is_drink_item) indicating business categorization
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_order_items, model.stg_orders, model.stg_products and writes model.order_items

domains.description: Core business domain focused on customer orders, revenue calculations, and item-level profitability analysis for sales reporting and financial insights.
domains.reasoning: These files share key concepts of order processing, revenue tracking, food/drink classification, and financial calculations. The marts (order_items, orders) and their corresponding staging models (stg_orders, stg_order_items) form a cohesive pipeline for order analytics, supported by the currency conversion macro.

domains.description: Domain focused on customer behavior analysis, lifetime value calculations, and customer relationship management for marketing and sales strategies.
domains.reasoning: These files form a complete customer data pipeline from staging to mart layer, with shared concepts of customer analytics, lifetime value, purchasing behavior, and customer relationship management.

domains.description: Domain handling product catalog management, supply chain costs, inventory tracking, and product profitability analysis.
domains.reasoning: These files share supply chain, inventory management, and product data concepts. The supplies and products models (both staging and mart) work together to track supply expenses, product information, and enable profitability analysis.

domains.description: Domain focused on geographic analysis, location performance metrics, and site-based business operations reporting.
domains.reasoning: These files form a complete location data pipeline with shared concepts of location analytics, geographic analysis, and performance metrics for business intelligence reporting.

domains.description: Cross-cutting domain providing foundational data infrastructure, time dimension modeling, and data governance capabilities.
domains.reasoning: These files provide infrastructure components: time dimension for analytics, schema organization for governance, source configuration, and project-level configuration. They support all business domains rather than focusing on specific business logic.

domains.description: Technical domain containing development workflow automation, code quality tools, and environment management configurations.
domains.reasoning: These are configuration files for development workflows (pre-commit hooks, task automation) with minimal business logic, focused on code quality and operational efficiency rather than business analytics.
